name: Estella - Irantzu Monastery Loop
name_original: Estella-Lezaun-Bearin – circular por tierras de Yerri
description: This difficult 34.5-mile mountain biking route in Sierra de Lóquiz offers challenging climbs and diverse landscapes with 5608 feet of elevat
region_intro: Mountain biking around Estella/Lizarra offers a diverse landscape for riders. The region features varied terrain, including natural parks like Urbasa-Andia, significant hills such as Montejurra, and forested areas. These physical features provide a range of challenges and scenic backdrops for mountain bike trails. The area's geography supports both demanding climbs and more flowing paths. Best mountain bike trails around Estella/Lizarra The most popular mountain bike trail is Estella - Parque Natural de Urbasa-Andia – circular por el cerro Garimendi, a 33.6 miles (54.1 km) trail that takes 5 hours 29 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the natural park and includes significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Estella - Irantzu Monastery Loop, a difficult 34.5 miles (55.6 km) path. This trail features substantial climbing and passes by the Irantzu Monastery. Local mountain bikers also love the Prison Bridge, Estella – Granada Tunnel loop from Estella-Lizarra, a 32.3 miles (51.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain including a historic bridge and tunnel, often completed in about 4 hours 9 minutes. Mountain biking around Estella/Lizarra is defined by natural parks, significant hills, and forested sections. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult mountain bike trails. The routes in Estella/Lizarra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Estella/Lizarra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Estella/Lizarra?
A: There are over 50 mountain bike trails around Estella/Lizarra. The region offers a diverse network of routes, catering to various skill levels and preferences.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for mountain biking in Estella/Lizarra?
A: The trails around Estella/Lizarra offer a range of difficulties. You'll find 4 easy routes, 43 moderate routes, and 9 difficult routes. This variety ensures options for both casual riders and those seeking a challenge.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Estella/Lizarra?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, the difficult route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987993174/estella-parque-natural-de-urbasa-andia-circular-por-el-cerro-garimendi" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estella - Parque Natural de Urbasa-Andia – circular por el cerro Garimendi&lt;/a&gt; offers a substantial loop through natural parkland. Another popular option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5346107" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prison Bridge, Estella – Granada Tunnel loop from Estella-Lizarra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Estella/Lizarra?
A: The terrain is diverse, featuring natural parks like Urbasa-Andia, significant hills such as Montejurra, and extensive forested areas. You can expect a mix of demanding climbs, flowing singletrack, and varied surfaces, providing a dynamic riding experience.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see while mountain biking?
A: Many trails offer scenic views and pass by interesting landmarks. You might encounter the historic Irantzu Monastery on routes like the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988012606/estella-irantzu-monastery-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estella - Irantzu Monastery Loop&lt;/a&gt;. Other highlights include the summit of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4428728" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montejurra&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4459613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Irantzu River Canyon&lt;/a&gt;, and various viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4108046" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de Lokiz&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year for mountain biking in Estella/Lizarra?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Estella/Lizarra, with mild temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be warm, especially at midday, while winter might bring cooler temperatures and occasional rain or snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Estella/Lizarra?
A: While many routes feature significant climbs, there are 4 easy trails available that could be suitable for families or beginners. These routes typically have less elevation gain and are shorter in distance, offering a more relaxed experience.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Estella/Lizarra?
A: The mountain bike trails in Estella/Lizarra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 40 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the natural beauty of the parks, and the challenging climbs.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Estella/Lizarra offers 9 difficult mountain bike routes designed for experienced riders. These trails often feature substantial elevation gains and technical sections, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987993174/estella-parque-natural-de-urbasa-andia-circular-por-el-cerro-garimendi" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estella - Parque Natural de Urbasa-Andia – circular por el cerro Garimendi&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988012606/estella-irantzu-monastery-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estella - Irantzu Monastery Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in Estella/Lizarra?
A: Estella/Lizarra, as a regional hub, offers various parking options within the town itself. Many trails start directly from or near the town center, or from smaller villages nearby. It's advisable to check the specific starting point of your chosen route for the most convenient parking facilities.

Q: Can I find trails that pass by water features or potential wild swimming spots?
A: While specific wild swimming spots are not explicitly listed for these mountain bike routes, the region is home to the Irantzu River Canyon (&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4459613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Irantzu River Canyon&lt;/a&gt;), which features water. Some trails may follow river valleys or pass by streams, offering scenic water views. Always check local regulations before attempting any wild swimming.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the trails?
A: Many routes start or pass through Estella/Lizarra itself, where you'll find numerous cafes, restaurants, and shops. For longer routes that venture into more remote areas, it's recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks, though some smaller villages along the way might offer limited refreshment options.
tips: A combined section of road and path that crosses the Peña de Martín beech forest, a wonderful landscape that varies depending on the time of year you visit it.
Path without technical difficulty that runs through the Irantzu river canyon, a wonderful landscape ideal to take advantage of in the summer months to take a refreshing bath in the river.

Sector composed of three very different sections. The first is very short and simple and we find it as soon as we leave Bearin, it serves as an aperitif for the second, La Misteriosa 2.0, which is longer and with some hairpin bends that must be taken with caution. After reaching the Regacho de Eraul, we face the last section uphill through a kind of tunnel between the trees on the shore.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Estella-Lezaun-Bearin mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This is a difficult mountain biking route, requiring very good physical condition. It features significant elevation gain (around 1,700 meters) and demanding terrain. While technically rated as medium difficulty, sections like the stony path through an oak grove near Lezaun can be quite challenging.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a variety of surfaces. The route starts from Estella and includes tough climbs, such as towards the Lizarraga pass after Lezaun. Sections follow the historic Camino de Santiago and the Cañada Real Andía-Tauste, offering well-maintained gravel paths. Be prepared for a particularly stony path through a beautiful oak grove on the way to Lezaun, which can be tricky. You'll also find 'legendary trails' like El Reagacho de Eraul and El Cirilo.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Estella-Lezaun-Bearin mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The circular route typically starts from Estella, where you should be able to find parking. As this is a popular cycling region, consider checking local information for specific trailhead parking areas in Estella or nearby towns like Lezaun or Bearin.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The route is beautiful year-round, but autumn is particularly scenic, especially with the vineyards of Bodegas Quaderna Vía. Spring and early summer also offer pleasant conditions. Always check the weather forecast, as conditions can change, especially in mountainous areas.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy diverse landscapes, including views of the Urbasa-Andía Natural Park, the Lókiz mountain range, and Montejurra. Notable landmarks include the Monasterio de Iratxe and its vineyards, the autumn vineyards of Bodegas Quaderna Vía, and the Embalse de Alloz. You'll also pass through charming fluvial parks, particularly near Abárzuza. Don't miss the Hayedo de la Peña de Martín and the Trail along the Irantzu River.

faqs.question: Is the Estella-Lezaun-Bearin mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in this region, but it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas, near livestock, or in natural parks like Sierra de Lóquiz. Given the trail's difficulty and length, ensure your dog is fit enough for the ride.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail or the Sierra de Lóquiz?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to ride this mountain bike trail or to access the general areas of Sierra de Lóquiz. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning natural parks and private land. The trail is part of a broader network of marked routes, which are generally free to access.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like bike repair or rentals available near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Tierra Estella region boasts a strong cycling tradition and offers support for riders. There are seven reception centers across the region that provide services such as workshops, repair kits, and bike rentals. You can find these in towns along or near the route.

faqs.question: Does the route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Camino de Elkez, Camino a Larraitza, Senda de Itolatz, Senda del Sacristán, and the Camino de Bedaga, among others. It also follows parts of the historic Camino de Santiago and the Cañada Real Andía-Tauste.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through or near several towns like Estella, Lezaun, and Bearin, you'll find various options for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation. Estella, being a larger town, will offer the most choices for pre- or post-ride refreshments and lodging.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length (over 55 km), you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes), a first-aid kit, and appropriate clothing for varying weather conditions. A GPS device or a fully charged phone with the route downloaded is also highly recommended.

waypoints.name: Hayedo de la Peña de Martín
